title:	Add Move and Delete operations for Epic Tasks
state:	OPEN
author:	byapparov
labels:	enhancement
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	572
--
## Problem

When restructuring an epic (e.g., extracting stories into separate epics), there's no way to move or delete tasks via MCP tools. This forced a workaround during ADMIN-1 restructuring where Story 4 (Billing) and Story 8 (Feature Flags) were extracted to BILL-1 and FLAG-1 respectively, but their tasks remain as deprecated stubs in ADMIN-1 because they can't be removed.

## Current Workaround

- Mark deprecated tasks in epic description with a "do not claim" note
- Leave orphaned tasks in the original epic
- This clutters the backlog and risks confusion

## Proposed Solution

Add classic task lifecycle operations:

### 1. Delete Task
- `delete_task(task_id)` — permanently remove a task from an epic
- Should cascade: remove associated features and acceptance criteria
- Should validate: task is not `active` or `in_progress` (safety check)

### 2. Move Task (subset of update?)
- `move_task(task_id, target_epic_id)` — move a task from one epic to another
- Preserves features, acceptance criteria, and evidence
- Updates dependency references (or warns if cross-epic deps would break)
- Alternative: could be an optional `epic_id` field on `update_task` rather than a separate operation

### 3. Update Task (enhance existing)
- Add `epic_id` field to allow re-parenting
- Add `status: "archived"` or `"removed"` for soft-delete

## Context

Discovered during ADMIN-1 epic restructuring where billing and feature flags were extracted into their own epics (BILL-1, FLAG-1). See tasks `f17a0d02` and `6be050c4` which are now deprecated but cannot be cleaned up.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tasks can be deleted from an epic via MCP tool
- [ ] Tasks can be moved between epics via MCP tool
- [ ] Deleted/moved tasks no longer appear in epic task counts
- [ ] Safety: cannot delete/move tasks that are actively claimed or in progress
- [ ] Dependencies are handled gracefully (warn or update references)

215: export async function handle(
216:   name: string,
217:   args: Record<string, unknown>,
218:   deps: ToolDependencies
219: ): Promise<ToolResponse> {
220:   const { stateManager, wsHandler, sessionId } = deps;
221: 
222:   // Read-only tools can work without a session by using the org-scoped MCP endpoint's orgId
223:   const readOnlyTools = ['list_epics', 'get_epic'];
224:   const session = sessionId ? await stateManager.getSession(sessionId) : null;
225: 
226:   const orgId = session?.organizationId || getCurrentOrgId();
227:   if (!orgId && !readOnlyTools.includes(name)) {
228:     return errorResponse(
229:       'no_organization',
230:       'No organization context. Either start a session (session_init) or use the org-scoped MCP endpoint.'
231:     );
232:   }
233: 
234:   const userId = getCurrentUserId() || 'anonymous';
235: 
236:   switch (name) {
237:     case 'create_epic': {
238:       const title = args.title as string;
239:       // Sanitize shortKey: remove non-alphabetic chars before validation
240:       const rawShortKey = args.short_key as string;
241:       const shortKey = rawShortKey.toUpperCase().replace(/[^A-Z]/g, '');
242:       const description = args.description as string | undefined;
243:       const priority = args.priority as 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'critical' | undefined;
244:       const tags = args.tags as string[] | undefined;
245: 
246:       // Validate shortKey format (after sanitization)
247:       if (!/^[A-Z]{2,10}$/.test(shortKey)) {
248:         return errorResponse(
249:           'invalid_short_key',
250:           'short_key must be 2-10 uppercase letters (e.g., "AUTH", "DATA")'
251:         );
252:       }
253: 
254:       if (!orgId) {
255:         return errorResponse('no_organization', 'Organization context required to create an epic.');
256:       }
257: 
258:       try {
259:         const epic = await stateManager.createEpic(orgId, {
260:           title,
261:           shortKey,
262:           description,
263:           priority,
264:           tags,
265:           source: 'mcp',
266:         }, userId);
267: 
268:         // Auto-link session to the new Epic
269:         if (sessionId && session) {
270:           await stateManager.linkSessionToEpic(sessionId, epic.id, session.mode || 'develop');
271:         }
272: 
273:         // Broadcast event
274:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:created', epic);
275: 
276:         return jsonResponse({
277:           success: true,
278:           epic_id: epic.id,
279:           title: epic.title,
280:           status: epic.status,
281:           message: `Epic ${epic.id} created and session linked. Use create_task to add tasks.`,
282:         });
283:       } catch (error) {
284:         return errorResponse(
285:           'create_failed',
286:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to create Epic'
287:         );
288:       }
289:     }
290: 
291:     case 'list_epics': {
292:       if (!orgId) {
293:         return errorResponse(
294:           'no_organization',
295:           'No organization context. Either start a session (session_init) or use the org-scoped MCP endpoint.'
296:         );
297:       }
298:       const status = args.status as EpicStatus | undefined;
299: 
300:       try {
301:         // Use paginated query with limit to prevent context overflow
302:         const result = await stateManager.listEpicSummariesPaginated(orgId, {
303:           status,
304:           limit: 50,
305:         });
306: 
307:         return jsonResponse({
308:           epics: result.items.map(e => ({
309:             id: e.id,
310:             title: e.title,
311:             status: e.status,
312:             source: e.source,
313:             tasks: {
314:               total: e.tasksTotal,
315:               available: e.tasksAvailable,
316:               in_progress: e.tasksInProgress,
317:               completed: e.tasksCompleted,
318:             },
319:             created_at: e.createdAt,
320:           })),
321:           total: result.items.length,
322:           pagination: {
323:             has_more: result.pagination.hasMore,
324:             next_cursor: result.pagination.nextCursor,
325:             hint: result.pagination.hasMore
326:               ? 'Showing first 50 epics. Full pagination support with limit/cursor parameters coming soon. Use status filter to narrow results.'
327:               : undefined,
328:           },
329:           filters: { status },
330:         });
331:       } catch (error) {
332:         return errorResponse(
333:           'list_failed',
334:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to list Epics'
335:         );
336:       }
337:     }
338: 
339:     case 'get_epic': {
340:       const epicId = args.epic_id as string;
341: 
342:       try {
343:         const epic = await stateManager.getEpic(epicId);
344:         if (!epic) {
345:           return errorResponse('not_found', `Epic ${epicId} not found`);
346:         }
347: 
348:         const tasks = await stateManager.listTasks(epicId);
349: 
350:         // Batch get feature counts for all tasks (avoids N+1 queries)
351:         const taskIds = tasks.map((t) => t.id);
352:         const featuresByTask = await stateManager.listFeaturesForTasks(taskIds);
353: 
354:         const tasksWithFeatureCounts = tasks.map((t) => {
355:           const features = featuresByTask.get(t.id) || [];
356:           return {
357:             id: t.id,
358:             title: t.title,
359:             description: t.description,
360:             status: t.status,
361:             claimed_by_session: t.claimedBySessionId,
362:             depends_on: t.dependsOn,
363:             complexity: t.estimatedComplexity,
364:             features_count: features.length,
365:           };
366:         });
367: 
368:         return jsonResponse({
369:           id: epic.id,
370:           title: epic.title,
371:           description: epic.description,
372:           status: epic.status,
373:           priority: epic.priority,
374:           source: epic.source,
375:           source_ref: epic.sourceRef,
376:           tags: epic.tags,
377:           created_by: epic.createdBy,
378:           owner_id: epic.ownerId,
379:           created_at: epic.createdAt,
380:           updated_at: epic.updatedAt,
381:           tasks: tasksWithFeatureCounts,
382:         });
383:       } catch (error) {
384:         return errorResponse(
385:           'get_failed',
386:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to get Epic'
387:         );
388:       }
389:     }
390: 
391:     case 'update_epic': {
392:       const epicId = args.epic_id as string;
393:       const updates: Record<string, unknown> = {};
394: 
395:       if (args.title) updates.title = args.title;
396:       if (args.description) updates.description = args.description;
397:       if (args.status) updates.status = args.status;
398:       if (args.priority) updates.priority = args.priority;
399: 
400:       if (Object.keys(updates).length === 0) {
401:         return errorResponse('no_updates', 'No updates provided');
402:       }
403: 
404:       try {
405:         const epic = await stateManager.updateEpic(epicId, updates as Parameters<typeof stateManager.updateEpic>[1]);
406: 
407:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:updated', epic);
408: 
409:         return jsonResponse({
410:           success: true,
411:           epic_id: epic.id,
412:           updated: Object.keys(updates),
413:         });
414:       } catch (error) {
415:         return errorResponse(
416:           'update_failed',
417:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to update Epic'
418:         );
419:       }
420:     }
421: 
422:     case 'create_task': {
423:       const epicId = args.epic_id as string;
424:       const title = args.title as string;
425:       const description = args.description as string | undefined;
426:       const dependsOn = args.depends_on as string[] | undefined;
427:       const complexity = args.complexity as TaskComplexity | undefined;
428:       const tags = args.tags as string[] | undefined;
429: 
430:       // Verify epic exists
431:       const epic = await stateManager.getEpic(epicId);
432:       if (!epic) {
433:         return errorResponse('epic_not_found', `Epic ${epicId} not found`);
434:       }
435: 
436:       try {
437:         const task = await stateManager.createTask({
438:           epicId,
439:           title,
440:           description,
441:           dependsOn,
442:           estimatedComplexity: complexity,
443:           tags,
444:         });
445: 
446:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:task:created', { epicId, task });
447: 
448:         return jsonResponse({
449:           success: true,
450:           task_id: task.id,
451:           epic_id: epicId,
452:           title: task.title,
453:           status: task.status,
454:           message: 'Task created. Use claim_task to start working on it.',
455:         });
456:       } catch (error) {
457:         return errorResponse(
458:           'create_failed',
459:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to create task'
460:         );
461:       }
462:     }
463: 
464:     case 'claim_task': {
465:       const taskId = args.task_id as string;
466: 
467:       try {
468:         // Check if task can be claimed (status gate)
469:         const claimCheck = await canClaimTask(stateManager, taskId);
470:         if (!claimCheck.canClaim) {
471:           // Provide helpful error with next action
472:           if (claimCheck.task?.status === 'draft') {
473:             return workflowErrorResponse(
474:               'status_gate_blocked',
475:               claimCheck.reason || 'Task cannot be claimed',
476:               {
477:                 tool: 'check_readiness',
478:                 description: 'Check task readiness criteria',
479:                 example_params: { task_id: taskId },
480:               },
481:               {
482:                 current_status: claimCheck.task.status,
483:                 required_status: 'ready',
484:                 hint: 'A human must approve the requirements and mark the task as "ready" before AI can claim it.',
485:               }
486:             );
487:           }
488:           return errorResponse(
489:             'claim_blocked',
490:             claimCheck.reason || 'Task cannot be claimed'
491:           );
492:         }
493: 
494:         const task = await stateManager.claimTask(taskId, sessionId);
495: 
496:         if (!task) {
497:           return errorResponse(
498:             'already_claimed',
499:             'Task is already claimed by another session. Use list_epics or get_epic to see available tasks.'
500:           );
501:         }
502: 
503:         // Also set this as the session's current task
504:         await stateManager.setSessionTask(sessionId, taskId);
505: 
506:         // Update task status to active when claimed
507:         await stateManager.updateTask(taskId, { status: 'active' });
508: 
509:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:task:claimed', { taskId, claimedBy: sessionId });
510: 
511:         return jsonResponse({
512:           success: true,
513:           task_id: task.id,
514:           title: task.title,
515:           status: 'active',
516:           message: 'Task claimed and now active! Create features with update_features to track deliverables.',
517:         });
518:       } catch (error) {
519:         return errorResponse(
520:           'claim_failed',
521:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to claim task'
522:         );
523:       }
524:     }
525: 
526:     case 'release_task': {
527:       const taskId = args.task_id as string;
528: 
529:       try {
530:         const task = await stateManager.getTask(taskId);
531:         if (!task) {
532:           return errorResponse('not_found', `Task ${taskId} not found`);
533:         }
534: 
535:         // Only allow releasing if this session claimed it
536:         if (task.claimedBySessionId !== sessionId) {
537:           return errorResponse(
538:             'not_owner',
539:             'Cannot release a task claimed by another session'
540:           );
541:         }
542: 
543:         const released = await stateManager.releaseTask(taskId);
544: 
545:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:task:updated', { taskId, status: 'ready' });
546: 
547:         return jsonResponse({
548:           success: true,
549:           task_id: released.id,
550:           status: released.status,
551:           message: 'Task released and available for other sessions.',
552:         });
553:       } catch (error) {
554:         return errorResponse(
555:           'release_failed',
556:           error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to release task'
557:         );
558:       }
559:     }
560: 
561:     case 'force_release_task': {
562:       const taskId = args.task_id as string;
563: 
564:       try {
565:         const task = await stateManager.getTask(taskId);
566:         if (!task) {
567:           return errorResponse('not_found', `Task ${taskId} not found`);
568:         }
569: 
570:         if (!task.claimedBySessionId) {
571:           return errorResponse('not_claimed', 'Task is not claimed by any session');
572:         }
573: 
574:         const previousSessionId = task.claimedBySessionId;
575:         const released = await stateManager.forceReleaseTask(taskId);
576: 
577:         if (!released) {
578:           return errorResponse('release_failed', 'Failed to release task');
579:         }
580: 
581:         wsHandler.broadcast(sessionId, 'epic:task:updated', { taskId, status: 'ready' });
582:

   - Safety checks: rejects if `active`, `review`, or claimed
   - Validates same-organization constraint
   - Logs warning for cross-epic dependencies
   - Returns task_id, source_epic_id, target_epic_id, features_moved

   - Added `moveTask(taskId, targetEpicId): Promise<EpicTask>`
   - Added optional `deletedBy` param to `deleteTask`

   - Uses batched writes for atomic updates
   - Updates task and all features atomically

   - Added `epic:task:deleted`, `epic:task:moved`
